title: MakeLabs — Elena Kelareva call
context: A phone call about MakeLabs, a proposed network of regional-Victorian community workshops (STEAM 'learn by doing' spaces; local nodes are '<Town> MakeLab', e.g. Bendigo MakeLab). Nick and Alexar are picking Elena Kelareva's brain on regional tech/maker initiatives — funding, mentors, gender-inclusive design, getting young regional people engaged. Alexar leads the concept pitch; Nick frames why they're talking to her and steers; Elena gives the unvarnished regional read from her GippsTech / Startup Gippsland experience. (This concept was earlier explored as 'BIT Centers' — that name is retired; it is MakeLabs now.)

speakers.name: Elena
speakers.profile: Elena Kelareva (She/Her) — CEO/Founder of GippsTech and Startup Gippsland, lives in Gippsland ~2h from Melbourne, runs the Binary Shift conference. The expert being consulted. Speaks from lived regional experience: funding mixes, why community hubs are hard to make financially viable, partnering with neighbourhood houses / Learn Local / local council for peppercorn rent, gender-inclusive design so regional women don't self-exclude, mentor retention. Measured, reflective, gives caveated real-world detail. Ran a co-working space called 'the Herd'.

speakers.name: Alexar
speakers.profile: Melbourne-based co-founder. Leads the MakeLabs concept pitch — the reverse-pump idea for regional towns, themed fortnights, Hackers on the Train. Energetic, storytelling, builds analogies (complex adaptive systems). Drives the vision; asks Elena to poke holes in it. Wants the concept framed as MakeLabs, not the old 'BIT Centers' name.

speakers.name: Nick
speakers.profile: Frames why they're talking to Elena and steers the conversation. Tells the story of starting a hackerspace in Toowoomba as a med student that's still running, now run by the young people who took it over. Draws out Elena's experience; interjects with clarifying questions.
